beats.reasoning: Light Rail is closed; walking within U-District is fastest. Must arrive by 11am for the match start time.

beats.reasoning: Convenient to stay at cafe for a quick meal after the match.

beats.activity: Wind down with a quick gaming session, then sleeps.

beats.activity: Sleeps deeply after a full day.

diary: Woke up early enough to catch the USA-Wales match at Solstice—grabbed some instant ramen first because, let's be real, that's my fuel. The cafe was packed with other fans and the energy was infectious, even though the result wasn't what we hoped for. Grabbed a pizza slice after and headed to the IMA for pickup basketball with the crew. My legs were definitely tired from the match-watching adrenaline, but it felt good to move around. Came home, played some games to recover, then had to buckle down at the library for a few hours on my CS assignments. The Light Rail was back up by the time I needed it, so that made the walk home easier. Stopped for my third energy drink of the day—don't judge—and now I'm winding down. It was one of those days where I felt genuinely present for most of it, you know?
thought_process: I prioritized the World Cup match because it's rare to feel that kind of collective excitement, especially on a Monday morning. The Light Rail closure didn't really impact me since I stick to walking in U-District anyway. Basketball was the perfect counterweight to sitting around watching the game—I needed to burn off that restless energy. My mood stayed pretty steady throughout; the match didn't disappoint me too much, and moving my body helped. By evening, I knew I had to hit the library hard for my coursework, so I paced myself with the gaming and energy drinks. It was a balanced day—social, physical, and productive. The fact that everything flowed without major friction made it feel almost effortless.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
